brief_title: Understanding Quality of Life Among Patients With Cancer Receiving Palliative Care
official_title: Understanding Quality of Life Among Patients With Cancer Receiving Palliative Care
brief_summary: This randomized controlled trial will assess the effects on quality of life of two approaches to symptom management among new patients referred to a palliative care oncology clinic.

interventions.name: Offering Additional Resources to Promote Symptom Management
interventions.description: Participants will be offered additional resources to promote symptom management.

eligibility.criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Be a new patient at an outpatient palliative care clinic
* Resident of Pennsylvania
* Over 18 years of age

Exclusion Criteria:

* Prior use of certain symptom focused therapies
* Currently pregnant

primary_outcomes.measure: Quality of Life - McGill Quality of Life Questionnaire
primary_outcomes.description: Quality of life will be measured through the McGill Quality of Life Questionnaire Revised (MQOL-R). The questionnaire is 15 questions assessing overall and specific areas of quality of life.

sponsor: Abramson Cancer Center at Penn Medicine
